sections.name: Information Gathering
items: Fingerprint web server (Server header, error pages, default files)
Enumerate application entry points (all URLs, forms, APIs)
Identify technologies (CMS, frameworks, libraries, versions)
Map authentication mechanisms (login, OAuth, SSO, MFA)
Identify all user roles and privilege levels
Discover hidden files and directories (ffuf, gobuster)
Review robots.txt, sitemap.xml, security.txt
Check for exposed .git, .svn, .env files
Search for backup files (.bak, .old, .zip, .tar.gz)
Review JavaScript source for secrets and endpoints

sections.name: Authentication Testing
items: Test for default credentials
Test account lockout policy (brute force protection)
Test password complexity requirements
Test password reset flow for weaknesses
Test 'Remember Me' functionality
Test session fixation
Test concurrent session handling
Test authentication bypass (direct URL access, parameter tampering)
Test OAuth/SSO implementation for common flaws
Verify MFA implementation and bypass possibilities

sections.name: Session Management
items: Inspect session token entropy and randomness
Verify HttpOnly flag on session cookies
Verify Secure flag on session cookies
Verify SameSite attribute (Lax or Strict)
Test session expiration (idle + absolute timeout)
Test logout functionality (server-side session invalidation)
Test for CSRF in state-changing operations
Check cookie scope (Domain and Path attributes)
Test for session token in URL parameters

sections.name: Access Control
items: Test horizontal privilege escalation (user A accessing user B's data)
Test vertical privilege escalation (regular user → admin functions)
Test for IDOR in all direct object references
Test forced browsing to restricted pages
Verify server-side access control enforcement (not just client-side)
Test API endpoints with different roles
Test parameter tampering (user_id, account_id, role)
Test directory traversal
Verify function-level access control

sections.name: Input Validation & Injection
items: Test all input fields for SQL injection (manual + sqlmap)
Test for stored, reflected, and DOM XSS
Test for SSTI (Server-Side Template Injection)
Test file upload for unrestricted file upload
Test for SSRF in URL/callback parameters
Test for XML injection and XXE
Test for LDAP injection in authentication
Test for HTTP header injection (Host, X-Forwarded-For)
Test for open redirect vulnerabilities
Test for command injection in OS-facing features

sections.name: Cryptography
items: Verify HTTPS on all pages (especially login/checkout)
Test SSL/TLS configuration (sslyze)
Check for mixed content (HTTP resources on HTTPS pages)
Verify secure cookie transmission
Check password storage mechanism (bcrypt preferred)
Test for sensitive data in HTTP responses/logs
Verify JWT implementation (algorithm confusion, weak secrets)

sections.name: Business Logic
items: Test price/quantity manipulation in e-commerce flows
Test workflow bypass (skip steps in multi-step processes)
Test for negative values, zero values, very large values
Test race conditions in sensitive operations
Test mass assignment vulnerabilities
Test feature flag bypass
Test email/domain validation bypass

sections.name: Error Handling & Information Disclosure
items: Force errors and review messages for stack traces
Check HTTP response headers for version information
Review error pages for technology disclosure
Test for verbose error messages in API responses
Check for sensitive data in HTTP logs and referrers
Review HTML source comments for sensitive information

sections.name: API Discovery
items: Map all API endpoints (Swagger/OpenAPI docs, JS files, traffic analysis)
Identify API version(s) in use
Enumerate HTTP methods supported by each endpoint
Identify authentication mechanisms (API keys, JWT, OAuth2)
Find deprecated API versions still accessible

sections.name: Authentication & Authorization
items: Test API key strength and entropy
Test JWT implementation (none algorithm, RS256→HS256 confusion, weak secrets)
Test OAuth2 flows for common vulnerabilities
Test BOLA/IDOR on all resource endpoints
Test function-level authorization (user performing admin operations)
Test for missing authentication on sensitive endpoints
Test API key rotation and revocation

sections.name: Rate Limiting & Resource Management
items: Test for missing rate limiting (BFLA)
Test for resource exhaustion (pagination without limits)
Test for unrestricted data exposure in responses
Test for mass assignment in POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints
Test GraphQL introspection and query depth limits

sections.name: Input Validation
items: Test all parameters for injection vulnerabilities
Test content type validation (JSON, XML, multipart)
Test parameter pollution (duplicate params)
Test for NoSQL injection (MongoDB, etc.)
Test for GraphQL injection
Test file upload endpoints

sections.name: Discovery
items: Port scan all in-scope IPs (TCP + UDP common ports)
Identify all live hosts (ping sweep, ARP scan)
Enumerate services and versions on open ports
Identify operating systems via fingerprinting
Map network topology and segments

sections.name: Service Enumeration
items: Enumerate SMB shares and permissions
Test RDP for weak configurations and NLA enforcement
Enumerate SNMP community strings
Test SSH for weak algorithms and version vulnerabilities
Enumerate FTP/SFTP for anonymous access
Test SMTP for open relay
Enumerate DNS for zone transfers
Test VPN endpoints and configurations

sections.name: Vulnerability Assessment
items: Run vulnerability scanner against all in-scope hosts
Check for EternalBlue/MS17-010 (SMB)
Check for BlueKeep/CVE-2019-0708 (RDP)
Check for Log4Shell/CVE-2021-44228
Check for known unpatched CVEs on identified software
Test for default credentials on all services
Check network device configurations (routers, firewalls, switches)

sections.name: Exploitation & Post-Exploitation
items: Exploit confirmed vulnerabilities with authorization
Test for credential reuse across systems
Test lateral movement possibilities
Test privilege escalation on compromised hosts
Assess network segmentation effectiveness
Test for sensitive data exposure on file shares
